2604.18701.title: Curiosity-Critic: Cumulative Prediction Error Improvement as a Tractable Intrinsic Reward for World Model Training
2604.18701.paper_summary: Local prediction-error-based curiosity rewards focus on the current transition without considering the world model's cumulative prediction error across all visited transitions. We introduce Curiosity-Critic, which grounds its intrinsic reward in the improvement of this cumulative objective, and show that it reduces to a tractable per-step form: the difference between the current prediction error and the asymptotic error baseline of the current state transition. We estimate this baseline online with a learned critic co-trained alongside the world model; regressing a single scalar, the critic converges well before the world model saturates, redirecting exploration toward learnable transitions without oracle knowledge of the noise floor. The reward is higher for learnable transitions and collapses toward the baseline for stochastic ones, effectively separating epistemic (reducible) from aleatoric (irreducible) prediction error online. Prior prediction-error curiosity formulations, from Schmidhuber (1991) to learned-feature-space variants, emerge as special cases corresponding to specific approximations of this baseline. Experiments on a stochastic grid world show that Curiosity-Critic outperforms prediction-error and visitation-count baselines in convergence speed and final world model accuracy.
2604.18701.paper_summary_zh: 當前基於預測誤差的好奇心獎勵專注於當前的轉換，而不考慮世界模型在所有訪問過的轉換中的累積預測誤差。我們引入了好奇心評論者（Curiosity-Critic），其內在獎勵基於這一累積目標的改進，並顯示它簡化為可處理的每步形式：當前預測誤差與當前狀態轉換的漸近誤差基準之間的差異。我們通過與世界模型共同訓練的學習評論者在線估計這一基準；評論者回歸一個單一的標量，在世界模型飽和之前就能很好地收斂，將探索重定向到可學習的轉換，而無需對噪聲底線的先驗知識。對於可學習的轉換，獎勵更高，而對於隨機的轉換則趨向於基準，有效地在線區分可約的（可減少的）與不可約的（不可減少的）預測誤差。從Schmidhuber（1991）到學習特徵空間變體的先前預測誤差好奇心公式，作為對應於這一基準特定近似的特例出現。在隨機網格世界上的實驗顯示，好奇心評論者在收斂速度和最終世界模型準確性方面超越了預測誤差和訪問計數基準。
2604.18701.author: Vin Bhaskara et.al.
